objective: Turn the Lumyn OSS MVP PRD into governed implementation slices for a local-first CLI.

evidence_summary.summary: The full MVP requires record/contract/replay/report, live verify/boundaries/CI, and live agent eval.
The MVP is not complete until all acceptance groups pass.
Deterministic bootstrap cannot use live credentials or network without human approval.

Factory delivery now requires local validation, CI/status checks, declared review posture, commit-push evidence, post-merge monitoring, and PR lifecycle evidence before product implementation continues; structured review is only required when risk, validation findings, pre-release posture, or review policy activates it.

Lumyn must preserve CI lanes, the 12-level test matrix, CodeQL risk-lane scanner posture, docs parity, output contracts, release integrity, provenance evidence, systems-thinking, TDD/ADR, performance, reliability, trust-mode, and fail-closed architecture rules in task packets and validation evidence before T3 product implementation continues.

Factory autoship-supervisor may supervise exactly one selected factoryd autoship task from the Lumyn repo root. factoryd remains the implementation and shipping engine; supervisor evidence is recorded as autoship_supervisor_report under .factory/artifacts/supervisor-runs/<task_id>/ when the skill is explicitly used.

assumptions: Use Go 1.26.4 for the CLI/core runtime.
Distribute as a standalone binary first, then Homebrew; PyPI is not a primary channel for Go Lumyn.
Use OpenAI-compatible HTTP including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters behind the live eval provider interface when eval work starts.
Keep the initial implementation deterministic and local-only.
Treat live sandbox verification and live eval as required MVP work, but blocked until credential and network posture are explicitly approved.
Keep Factory delivery lifecycle evidence separate from Lumyn product artifacts.

assumptions.value: CodeQL runs in GitHub Actions; local validation proves workflow presence and shape, while PR lifecycle evidence observes the remote status check.

alignment_questions.question: Should Lumyn remain a Python CLI for the MVP or switch to Go/Node before implementation begins?
alignment_questions.default_assumption: Go 1.26.4 CLI, standalone binary first, Homebrew later.

alignment_questions.question: Which provider adapters should ship for MVP eval?
alignment_questions.default_assumption: OpenAI-compatible HTTP including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters behind a provider interface.

alignment_questions.question: What sandbox key source and network allowlist should be approved for live verify?
alignment_questions.default_assumption: No live credentials during deterministic bootstrap; approve sandbox/model/network later.

scope_risks: The MVP is broad because live verify, CI, share, and eval are all required before closure.
Credential brokering is not needed for deterministic bootstrap but becomes blocking for live verify and eval.
Redaction must be designed before any trace/cassette/report persistence is trusted.
T1 and T2 used local validation and manual PR shipping; T2.5 closes the missing delivery-lifecycle baseline before T3.
Model provider endpoint work requires a model_provider_endpoint grant; generic network or credential approval does not satisfy live eval dispatch.

runtime.dependency_policy: standard library only for T1; add pinned dependencies only when required by later tasks

live_work_policy.model_provider_endpoint_gate: OpenAI-compatible/custom base_url and Anthropic live eval provider endpoint work requires an approved model_provider_endpoint grant; generic network or credential approval is not sufficient.

mvp_eval_provider_adapters.sdk_policy: no heavy SDK dependency for MVP bootstrap; use thin HTTP adapters unless a later task justifies a pinned SDK
mvp_eval_provider_adapters.network_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and a complete model_provider_endpoint grant approves provider identity, provider model, endpoint/base_url, credential environment, budget posture, redaction posture, and network allowlist

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

alignment_decisions.factoryd_dispatch_policy: runner-ready task packets must declare worker_type, factoryd_runtime, validation_commands, max_iterations, evidence_required, stop_conditions, path policy, lifecycle gates, and scope exclusions before dispatch

model_provider_endpoint.value: OpenAI-compatible/custom base_url and Anthropic live eval provider endpoint work is required MVP scope but remains blocked until an operator approves provider endpoint metadata and credential/network posture through a model_provider_endpoint grant.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

blocking_decisions: distribution: standalone binary first, Homebrew later, PyPI not primary
provider: OpenAI-compatible HTTP including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters for eval, no SDK-heavy dependency during deterministic bootstrap
artifact namespace: Factory evidence in .factory/artifacts/ and product artifacts in workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/
credential and live-work posture: no network, sandbox credentials, or model keys until deterministic replay passes and human approval unlocks them
dependency policy: standard library first; pinned dependencies only when required and justified

drift_triggers: Factory contract, profile, skill inventory, or skill vocabulary changes that alter required fields, lifecycle workers, validation gates, or evidence expectations
